subject: Geology
title: Desalination and Water Scarcity
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Desalination and Water Scarcity.

hook.text: The planet is 70 percent water, yet billions face shortages. Almost all of that water is salty or frozen.

buildup.text: Only about 1 percent of Earth's water is fresh and accessible. Growing demand and climate change are tightening supply.

discovery.text: Desalination removes salt from seawater. Reverse osmosis pushes water through membranes, leaving salt behind.

twist.text: Desalination is energy-intensive and produces brine waste. The geology of coastal aquifers can be harmed by intake.

climax.text: Water scarcity is a geologic and human challenge. Solutions must balance engineering, energy, and natural systems.

punchline.text: Plenty of water exists, just not fresh.

quiz.question: How does reverse osmosis desalination work?
options: It boils seawater and collects steam
It pushes water through membranes that filter out salt
It freezes seawater and skims off ice
